caption:
Everyone wants AI to draft the PRD. Fewer teams ask when a human should still own it.

I use Claude for first drafts — but I won't hand it the PRD when:

→ The call is political, not technical (reorg, sunset, pricing — someone has to own the narrative)
→ We're choosing what NOT to build (tradeoffs need a name, not a polished paragraph)
→ Legal, trust, or data risk is on the table (the model will sound confident while being wrong)
→ We haven't talked to a user in two weeks (no quotes in thread = generic output)

That's not being anti-AI. It's refusing to hide behind a doc when the job is judgment.

AI helps when the decision is already made and you're packaging it.

It hurts when "we'll run it through the model" replaces a tense conversation.

Tuesday's post was what I wire before AI writes.
This is what I still write myself.

What PRD section do you never outsource — and why?

#productmanagement #artificialintelligence #leadership
